title: Housing costs
description: Practice understanding of total housing costs

assessment_items.question_content:
When you are thinking about how much to spend on housing, you have to consider rent or mortgage and cost of *utilities*.

**Which of these expenses are *utilities*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Utilities are essential *services* that are necessary for daily living and functioning.
Utilities typically have a monthly bill that you have to pay to keep receiving the service.
While food is necessary for living, groceries represent *items*, not services you pay for.
**Internet** and **electricity** are utilities.

assessment_items.question_content:
When you are thinking about how much to spend on housing, you have to consider rent or mortgage and cost of *utilities*.

**Which of these expenses are *utilities*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Utilities are essential *services* that are necessary for daily living and functioning.
Utilities typically have a monthly bill that you have to pay to keep receiving the service.
While some form of clothing is considered necessary for daily living, they are not services you pay for.
**Natural gas** and **water** are utilities.

assessment_items.question_content:
When you are thinking about how much to spend on housing, you have to consider rent or mortgage and cost of *utilities*.

**Which of these expenses are *utilities*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Utilities are essential *services* that are necessary for daily living and functioning.
Utilities typically have a monthly bill that you have to pay to keep receiving the service.
While homeowners insurance is necessary when you have a mortgage, it is not a service required for daily living.
**Water** and **electricity** are utilities.

assessment_items.question_content:
Your housing budget is $\$1{,}400$ per month and these are expenses you estimate you will have:

**Expense** | **Monthly Cost**
:-: | :-:
Electricity | $\$100$
Internet | $\$50$
Water | $\$30$
Renter's insurance | $\$20$
**Total** | **$\$200$**

**How much can you afford for rent?**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Housing budget represents the **total** amount you should be paying for rent, utilities, and any other housing expenses.
To find out how much we can afford for rent, we subtract the total of housing expenses from the housing budget.
Total rent you can afford is $\$$ $\boxed{1{,}200}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Your housing budget is $\$1{,}800$ per month. The lease specifies that you will pay some utilities, but the landlord will be responsible for the water bill. These are the expenses you estimate you will have:

**Expense** | **Monthly Cost**
:-: | :-:
Electricity | $\$120$
Garbage collection | $\$70$
Water | $\$0$
Phone service | $\$30$
**Total** | **$\$220$**

**How much can you afford for rent?**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Housing budget represents the **total** amount you should be paying for rent, utilities, and any other housing expenses.
To find out how much we can afford for rent, we subtract the total of housing expenses from the housing budget.
Total rent you can afford is $\$$ $\boxed{{1{,}580}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Your housing budget is $\$2{,}100$ per month. The apartment you are interested in states it is all-bills paid. These are the expenses you estimate you will have:

**Expense** | **Monthly Cost**
:-: | :-:
Additional parking spot | $\$80$
Gas and electricity| $\$0$
Water | $\$0$
Renter's insurance | $\$20$
**Total** | **$\$100$**

**How much can you afford for rent?**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Housing budget represents the **total** amount you should be paying for rent, utilities, and any other housing expenses.
To find out how much we can afford for rent, we subtract the total of housing expenses from the housing budget.
Total rent you can afford is $\$$ $\boxed{2{,}000}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Your housing budget is $\$1{,}200$. The condo you are interested in renting has the following  expense estimates:

**Expense** | **Monthly Cost**
:-: | :-:
Electricity | $\$120$
Internet | $\$50$
Water | $\$30$
Condo fees | $\$80$
**Total** | **$\$280$**

To help stay within your budget, you are considering getting a roommate to split all housing costs with.

**What should your portion of the rent be if you are splitting all housing costs with a roommate?**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Housing budget represents the **total** amount you should be paying for rent, utilities, and any other housing expenses.
To find out how much we can afford for rent, we subtract the total of housing expenses from the housing budget.

Since we are splitting expenses with a roommate, our portion of the housing expenses will be *half* of the total amount. 

$\$280 \div 2 = \$140$ in monthly housing expenses

We then subtract the monthly housing expenses from our budget:

$\$1{,}200 - \$140=\$1{,}060$
Our portion of the rent will be $\$$ $\boxed{1{,}060}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Your housing budget is $\$1{,}600$. The condo you are interested in renting has the following  expense estimates:

**Expense** | **Monthly Cost**
:-: | :-:

Gas | $\$50$
Electricity | $\$80$
Condo fees | $\$70$
**Total** | **$\$300$**

To help stay within your budget, you are considering getting a roommate to split all housing costs with.

**What should your portion of the rent be if you are splitting all housing costs with a roommate?**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Housing budget represents the **total** amount you should be paying for rent, utilities, and any other housing expenses.
To find out how much we can afford for rent, we subtract the total of housing expenses from the housing budget.

Since we are splitting expenses with a roommate, our portion of the housing expenses will be *half* of the total amount. 

$\$300 \div 2 = \$150$ in monthly housing expenses

We then subtract the monthly housing expenses from our budget:

$\$1{,}600 - \$150=\$1{,}450$
Our portion of the rent will be $\$$ $\boxed{1{,}450}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Your housing budget is $\$1{,}900$. The condo you are interested in renting has the following  expense estimates:

**Expense** | **Monthly Cost**
:-: | :-:
Heating oil | $\$90$
Garbage collection | $\$50$
Electricity | $\$80$
Satellite TV  | $\$100$
**Total** | **$\$320$**

To help stay within your budget, you are considering getting a roommate to split all housing costs with.

**What should your portion of the rent be if you are splitting all housing costs with a roommate?**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Housing budget represents the **total** amount you should be paying for rent, utilities, and any other housing expenses.
To find out how much we can afford for rent, we subtract the total of housing expenses from the housing budget.

Since we are splitting expenses with a roommate, our portion of the housing expenses will be *half* of the total amount. 

$\$320 \div 2 = \$160$ in monthly housing expenses

We then subtract the monthly housing expenses from our budget:

$\$1{,}900 - \$160=\$1{,}740$
Our portion of the rent will be $\$$ $\boxed{1{,}740}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is a list of monthly expenses for an apartment:

**Expense** | **Monthly cost**
:-: | :-:
Electricity | $\$90$
Internet | $\$60$
Water | $\$25$
Maintenance | $\$45$
Trash collection | $\$20$

**Calculate the total amount of expenses.**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]

**If the rent for this apartment is $\$1{,}600$, what is the total amount of housing expenses?**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: To calculate the total amount of expenses, we add up all the amounts.

$\begin{align} 90& \text{ electricity} \\
60& \text{ internet}\\
25& \text{ water}\\
45& \text{ maintenance}\\\underline{+~20}& \text{ trash collection}\\
240&
\end{align}$    

Total expenses are $\$$ $\boxed{240}$.

To find the total amount of housing expenses, we will add rent to the total expenses.

$\$240+\$1{,}600=\$1{,}840$
Total housing expenses are $\$$ $\boxed{1{,}840}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is a list of monthly expenses for an apartment:

**Expense** | **Monthly cost**
:-: | :-:
Heating oil | $\$80$
Cable TV | $\$100$
Water | $\$35$
Electricity | $\$120$
Sewage | $\$30$

**Calculate the total amount of expenses.**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]

**If the rent for this apartment is $\$1{,}900$, what is the total amount of housing expenses?**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: To calculate the total amount of expenses, we add up all the amounts.

$\begin{align} 80& \text{ heating oil} \\
100& \text{ cable tv }\\
35& \text{ water}\\
120& \text{ electricity}\\
\underline{+~30}& \text{ sewage}\\
365&
\end{align}$    

Total expenses are $\$$ $\boxed{365}$.

To find the total amount of housing expenses, we will add rent to the total expenses.

$\$365+\$1{,}900=\$2{,}265$
Total housing expenses are $\$$ $\boxed{2{,}265}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is a list of monthly expenses for an apartment:

**Expense** | **Monthly cost**
:-: | :-:
Natural gas | $\$70$
Internet service | $\$80$
Phone service | $\$30$
Electricity | $\$110$
Garbage collection | $\$40$

**Calculate the total amount of expenses.**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]

**If the rent for this apartment is $\$1{,}500$, what is the total amount of housing expenses?**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: To calculate the total amount of expenses, we add up all the amounts.

$\begin{align} 70& \text{ natural gas} \\
80& \text{ internet service }\\
30& \text{ telephone service}\\
110& \text{ electricity}\\
\underline{+~40}& \text{ garbage collection}\\
330&
\end{align}$    

Total expenses are $\$$ $\boxed{330}$.

To find the total amount of housing expenses, we will add rent to the total expenses.

$\$330+\$1{,}500=\$1{,}830$
Total housing expenses are $\$$ $\boxed{1{,}830}$.
